title: huperzia selago subsp. arctica
intro: Huperzia selago subsp. arctica, also known as the Arctic fir club moss, is a subspecies of evergreen, perennial plant that belongs to the Huperzia genus. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to inhibit the acetylcholinesterase enzyme, which may contribute to its neuroprotective effects. The health benefits of Huperzia selago subsp. arctica are believed to include improved cognitive function and potential protection against neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in capsules or teas, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
